speaker: Isabel Schnabel
tone_summary: Schnabel remains the most hawkish voice on the Executive Board based on the readable speech content. Her March 6 US Monetary Policy Forum speech directly pushes back against calls for a dual mandate and growth-oriented easing, arguing that central banks must maintain a credible commitment to price stability regardless of political pressure — a classically hawkish institutional stance. She explicitly warns against repeating the 1960s-70s mistake of subordinating monetary policy to fiscal objectives. Her February 'Made in Europe' speech is more structural/growth-focused, but even there she frames Europe's competitiveness challenge without advocating for easier monetary conditions. Two of her four speeches were only available as PDFs (unreadable), which introduces uncertainty — score is held stable rather than moved higher.
representative_quote: In a world marked by more frequent supply-side shocks, the main challenge of central banks, regardless of their mandate, is to preserve a credible commitment to price stability.
